---
title: "Final Project"
author: "Yasmin Guadarrama, Harsh Mishrikotkar, Shivanshi Shukla"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r load files}

data <- read.csv("Tetuan City power consumption.csv")

```

## Possible questions to address:

- How well can we predict Zone 1, Zone 2, and Zone 3 power consumption using temperature, humidity, wind speed, general diffuse flows, diffuse flows, and time information?
- Which variables are the most important drivers of power demand in each zone?
- Do different zones behave differently, meaning should we build separate models for each zone instead of one combined model?
- Does adding time based features, such as hour of day, day of week, or month, improve prediction accuracy beyond weather-only predictors?
- Which method gives the best tradeoff between accuracy and interpretability for short-term electricity demand forecasting?
